name: Central State Bank
style: one-story bank with broad arched parapet panels, stone-veneer piers, recessed glazing, and an attached drive-through canopy

floors.windowPattern: large dark-glazed openings separated by white rounded pilasters and a central stone-veneer pier

notes: Tan upper wall has repeated shallow white arched panels; blur and cropping prevent complete opening counts.

floors.windowPattern: largely blank side wall, partly obscured by a tree

notes: Broad dark drive-through canopy is carried by square tan columns with reddish-brown bases.
Identity comes from the mapped footprint and parcel context; bank wording is not readable in these frames.

name: Dunkin'
style: one-story drive-through restaurant with a raised sign tower, horizontal accent strips, and dark metal-panel side walls

notes: Beige tower has thin white, orange, and pink strips; adjoining side wall is charcoal vertical metal panel.
This is a side/rear elevation, not the public entrance.
The freestanding drive-through sign is not building-mounted signage.

name: Clewiston Marathon convenience store
style: one-story roadside gas-station convenience store behind a wide flat pump canopy

floors.windowPattern: dark storefront glazing below the fascia, obscured by vehicles and pump structures

notes: Pale walls have dark blue-gray fascia trim and narrow red accents; the fuel canopy obscures much of the facade.
The Marathon pole sign and fuel canopy are separate structures, not facade features.

name: McDonald's
style: one-story fast-food building with a charcoal facade, wood-look accent bays, red inset panels, and small white metal canopies

floors.windowPattern: two small white-framed windows in wood-look vertical bays with red panels above

notes: Visible side elevation is predominantly blank charcoal wall; the left window has a projecting white canopy.
Identity comes from the mapped building and parcel context; only the side elevation is documented.

name: 341 Central Avenue masonry service building
style: plain one-story masonry service building with a low-pitch ribbed-metal gable roof

floors.windowPattern: at least two narrow vertical windows; the complete edge is cropped

notes: Light beige masonry wall, white metal roof, medium-blue exterior door, and surface-mounted utilities are visible.
Camera geometry confirms the target despite the cropped facade.

name: 528 E Sugarland Highway real-estate office
style: one-story storefront office with tan stucco, stacked-stone veneer, white-framed glazing, and a deep red-brown metal parapet canopy

floors.windowPattern: large white-framed picture windows alternate with glazed entry doors; right endpoint is cropped

notes: Stacked multicolor stone veneer covers the lower wall below white belt trim; positions are approximate because the view is oblique.
Only the exact readable text “Real Estate” is retained; the cropped neighboring wording is not guessed.
